preamble: The transformation from an alveolar click [!] to a voiceless uvular stop [q]. This process, known as declickization, occurs when the front (anterior) click closure is lost, leaving the back (posterior) uvular closure as the primary articulation.

examples.note: The letter 'q' represents the alveolar click, reflecting its historical and phonetic connection to uvular articulation.
Historical loss of clicks in certain environments results in dorsal stops.

sources: Ladefoged, P., & Maddieson, I. (1996). The Sounds of the World's Languages.
Miller, A. L. (2011). The Phonetics of Clicks.
